yesterdayStoryline.headline: Morocco rattle Brazil; Qatar steal a point
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Switzerland paid for wastefulness, Scotland edged Haiti, and Australia punished Türkiye on the counter.

todayStoryline.headline: Group E opens with sharp contrasts
todayStoryline.subtitle: Germany face Curaçao's debut, while Netherlands-Japan and Sweden-Tunisia shape a tight Group F.

lines: Switzerland made most of the running, but wastefulness left Qatar alive deep into stoppage time.
The draw puts all four Group B teams level after one round.
Morocco pressed through Brazil's midfield early, forcing Ancelotti's side to chase the match.
Brazil avoid defeat, but Group C already looks less predictable than expected.
Haiti made the contest direct and awkward, but McGinn punished a rebound before the break.
Scotland lead Group C while Haiti leave with credit and no points.
Türkiye dominated the ball, but Australia defended compactly and countered with precision.
The result puts Australia behind the United States on goal difference in Group D.

facts.home: Germany's 2006 World Cup summer as hosts later curdled into a bid-scandal investigation around Franz Beckenbauer. A report could not rule out vote-buying, leaving a stain on summer-fairytale nostalgia.
facts.away: Curaçao's 2020 rebuild carried a cold cut: Remko Bicentini said he learned through media that Guus Hiddink would take his job. He later fumed big Dutch names arrived lecturing locals.

facts.home: The Netherlands' Euro 1996 camp imploded when Edgar Davids was sent home after blasting Guus Hiddink in a radio interview. Headlines framed a racial split; the quarter-final exit followed.
facts.away: Japan's 1998 debut began with a shock cut: Kazuyoshi Miura was dropped one day after scoring a hat-trick. King Kazu had carried qualifying, then never played a World Cup.

facts.home: Ivory Coast's 2005 qualification party became a peace broadcast when Didier Drogba begged combatants to drop weapons. The plea helped shift mood before the 2007 Bouaké reconciliation match.
facts.away: Ecuador's 2015 FIFAgate fallout reached Luis Chiriboga, the federation boss who was later jailed for money-laundering. FIFA then banned him for life and fined him one million Swiss francs.

facts.home: Sweden's 2018 World Cup defeat to Germany turned ugly when Jimmy Durmaz received racist abuse and death threats. The squad publicly closed ranks, reading an anti-racism statement together.
facts.away: Tunisia's 2022 AFCON opener descended into farce when referee Janny Sikazwe blew full-time early, twice. CAF dismissed Tunisia's complaint after players refused a chaotic attempted restart in Limbe.

news.headline: Trump to meet Middle East leaders at G7 summit
news.body: U.S. officials said Donald Trump will meet Middle Eastern leaders and attend a Ukraine working session with Volodymyr Zelenskyy during next week's G7 summit in France. They said no bilateral meeting with Zelenskyy was scheduled, though a sideline encounter remained possible.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
